channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: UN Youth4Disarmament Champions Programme 2026–2027: Fully Supported Global Opportunity for Young Peace and Security Leaders to Lead Disarmament Projects
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
Applications are now open for the third edition of the United Nations Youth4Disarmament Champions Programme 2026–2027, a prestigious global youth leadership initiative organized by the United Nations Office for Disarmament Affairs (UNODA). The programme is designed to empower young leaders who are passionate about peace, security, disarmament, arms control, and weapons management by equipping them […]

The post UN Youth4Disarmament Champions Programme 2026–2027: Fully Supported Global Opportunity for Young Peace and Security Leaders to Lead Disarmament Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the third edition of the United Nations Youth4Disarmament Champions Programme 2026–2027, a prestigious global youth leadership initiative organized by the United Nations Office for Disarmament Affairs (UNODA). The programme is designed to empower young leaders who are passionate about peace, security, disarmament, arms control, and weapons management by equipping them with the knowledge, mentorship, resources, and international exposure needed to lead meaningful change in their communities.

As part of the Youth4Disarmament Initiative, UNODA will select nine outstanding young people from around the world to become Youth4Disarmament Champions. Over the course of one year, participants will receive specialized training, personalized mentorship, networking opportunities, project funding support, and international exposure while implementing their own disarmament-focused projects.

The programme aims to amplify youth voices in global disarmament discussions and strengthen the role of young people in advancing international peace and security. By bringing together participants from diverse regions and backgrounds, Youth4Disarmament Champions contributes to a more inclusive and representative global conversation on arms control, non-proliferation, weapons management, and sustainable peace.

About the Youth4Disarmament Initiative

The Youth4Disarmament Initiative is a flagship programme of the United Nations Office for Disarmament Affairs that seeks to engage, educate, and empower young people to participate actively in disarmament and arms control processes.

Recognizing that young people are key stakeholders in building peaceful societies, the initiative creates opportunities for youth to contribute innovative ideas, influence policy discussions, and promote awareness of disarmament issues at local, national, regional, and international levels.

The Youth4Disarmament Champions Programme is one of the initiative’s most impactful leadership opportunities, providing selected participants with practical support to transform their ideas into action.

Why the Youth4Disarmament Champions Programme Matters

In an increasingly complex global security environment, issues such as armed violence, illicit weapons trafficking, small arms proliferation, ammunition management, and disarmament policy continue to affect communities worldwide.

Young people have an essential role to play in:

  • Promoting peace and security
  • Preventing violence
  • Supporting disarmament efforts
  • Encouraging responsible weapons management
  • Strengthening community resilience
  • Advancing the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)

The Youth4Disarmament Champions Programme empowers youth leaders to become active contributors to these efforts through practical projects and global engagement.

Number of Champions Selected

For the 2026–2027 cohort, UNODA will select:

Nine Youth4Disarmament Champions

The selected participants will represent a geographically diverse group of young leaders from:

  • Africa
  • Europe
  • Asia and the Pacific
  • The Americas
  • The Caribbean

This diversity ensures that the programme benefits from a broad range of perspectives, experiences, and local realities.

Programme Duration

The programme will run for approximately one year:

August 2026 – Summer 2027

Participants should be prepared to dedicate:

  • Approximately 5–10 hours per week

during the programme period.

Study visits and international meetings may require full-time participation for approximately one week.

Programme Components

The Youth4Disarmament Champions Programme offers a comprehensive leadership and professional development experience.

1. Specialized Training on Disarmament

Selected Champions will participate in:

  • Online courses
  • Expert-led webinars
  • Capacity-building sessions
  • Interactive learning modules

Training topics include:

  • Disarmament principles
  • Non-proliferation
  • Arms control
  • Weapons management
  • International peace and security
  • Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)
  • Project management

Participants will gain a deeper understanding of how disarmament intersects with sustainable development and global security.

2. Individual Mentorship

Each Champion will receive personalized mentorship from:

  • UNODA staff
  • UNODA-designated experts and mentors

Mentorship will help participants:

  • Refine project ideas
  • Develop leadership skills
  • Strengthen advocacy strategies
  • Expand professional knowledge
  • Build confidence in implementing projects

3. Youth-Led Disarmament Project

A central feature of the programme is the implementation of an individual project.

Each Champion must:

  • Design a project
  • Develop an implementation plan
  • Execute the project during the programme

Projects may focus on:

  • Peacebuilding
  • Arms control awareness
  • Weapons management
  • Community engagement
  • Youth advocacy
  • Security education
  • Policy awareness initiatives

Participants will have flexibility to tailor their projects to local needs and regional priorities.

4. Networking Opportunities

Champions will join a growing global network of:

  • Current Youth4Disarmament Champions
  • Previous Champions
  • UN officials
  • International experts
  • Peace and security practitioners

Networking opportunities help participants build lasting professional relationships and collaborations.

5. Participation in Global Meetings

Throughout the programme, Champions may be invited to:

  • International conferences
  • Intergovernmental meetings
  • Disarmament forums
  • Policy dialogues
  • Global youth consultations

These opportunities allow participants to share their experiences and perspectives on international platforms.

6. Special Opportunities for Small Arms and Ammunition Experts

Champions whose projects focus on:

  • Small arms
  • Light weapons
  • Ammunition management

may engage with processes connected to:

The First Meeting of States on the Framework on Conventional Ammunition Management

This provides a rare opportunity to participate in emerging global policy discussions.

Financial and Professional Benefits

Selected Champions will receive several benefits throughout the programme.

Project Support Funding

Participants will receive:

  • A stipend to support implementation of their disarmament project

OR

  • An opportunity to contribute to a disarmament-related publication

International Travel Opportunities

Champions will participate in:

  • At least one international trip

during the programme.

Travel may include attendance at:

  • Global disarmament meetings
  • Intergovernmental conferences
  • International policy events
  • Relevant peace and security forums

Professional Development

Participants gain:

  • Leadership training
  • Project management skills
  • Policy knowledge
  • Public speaking experience
  • International exposure
  • Professional networking opportunities

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet all eligibility requirements.

Age Requirement

Applicants must:

  • Be between 18 and 29 years old at the time of application

Academic and Professional Background

Applicants should possess:

  • Previous knowledge or experience in peace, security, disarmament, weapons management, or related fields

While extensive professional experience is not required, familiarity with these topics is important.

Project Idea Requirement

Applicants must:

  • Have a clear idea for a disarmament-related project

The project should be feasible and relevant to their community or region.

Motivation Requirement

Candidates should demonstrate:

  • Strong commitment to peace and security
  • Genuine interest in disarmament issues
  • Willingness to learn
  • Capacity to complete the programme

Language Requirement

Applicants must possess:

  • Good command of English
  • Minimum English proficiency level: B2

Selection Criteria

Applications will be evaluated based on:

Motivation

Assessors will examine:

  • Commitment to disarmament
  • Passion for peacebuilding
  • Interest in global security issues

Project Proposal Quality

Strong applications will present:

  • Clear objectives
  • Practical implementation plans
  • Potential community impact
  • Innovative approaches

Relevant Experience

Previous involvement in:

  • Peace initiatives
  • Youth engagement
  • Advocacy work
  • Security projects
  • Research activities

will be considered an asset.

Programme Supporters

The Youth4Disarmament Champions Programme is generously supported by:

Government of the Republic of Korea

The Government of the Republic of Korea provides support for the programme’s implementation and youth engagement efforts.

European Union

The European Union supports Youth Champions working on:

  • Small arms
  • Light weapons
  • Ammunition management

Why Young Leaders Should Apply

The Youth4Disarmament Champions Programme is one of the few United Nations initiatives dedicated specifically to empowering youth in the field of disarmament and international security.

Participants gain:

  • UN-level exposure
  • Personalized mentorship
  • Project funding support
  • International networking
  • Leadership development
  • Global advocacy opportunities
  • Real-world experience in peace and security

For young people committed to creating safer, more peaceful communities, this programme provides a unique platform to transform ideas into action while becoming part of a global movement for disarmament.

APPLY HERE

Visit HERE to learn more about the UN Youth4Disarmament Champions Programme 2026–2027.

Conclusion

The UN Youth4Disarmament Champions Programme 2026–2027 offers an exceptional opportunity for young peacebuilders, advocates, researchers, and emerging leaders to deepen their knowledge of disarmament while implementing meaningful projects in their communities. Through comprehensive training, expert mentorship, financial support, international travel opportunities, and engagement with global disarmament processes, the programme equips participants with the tools needed to drive positive change. As the world continues to face evolving security challenges, the voices, ideas, and leadership of young people are more important than ever. If you are passionate about peace, security, and creating a safer future, this United Nations programme could be your opportunity to make a lasting impact.

Discover more global Fellowship, Leadership and UN opportunities on OFY.

The post UN Youth4Disarmament Champions Programme 2026–2027: Fully Supported Global Opportunity for Young Peace and Security Leaders to Lead Disarmament Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNIDIR and CIGI Launch the Outer Space Security Conference 2026 Youth Video Competition: Win a Fully Funded Trip to Geneva, Switzerland
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
Young researchers and aspiring space innovators from around the world now have a unique opportunity to contribute to global conversations on the future of space security. The United Nations Institute for Disarmament Research (UNIDIR), in partnership with the Centre for International Governance Innovation (CIGI), has officially launched the Outer Space Security Conference 2026 (OS26) Youth […]

The post UNIDIR and CIGI Launch the Outer Space Security Conference 2026 Youth Video Competition: Win a Fully Funded Trip to Geneva, Switzerland appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young researchers and aspiring space innovators from around the world now have a unique opportunity to contribute to global conversations on the future of space security. The United Nations Institute for Disarmament Research (UNIDIR), in partnership with the Centre for International Governance Innovation (CIGI), has officially launched the Outer Space Security Conference 2026 (OS26) Youth Video Competition.

This prestigious international competition invites young people aged 18 to 25 to showcase their research, technical expertise, and innovative ideas on how emerging developments in space architecture can help address growing security and sustainability challenges in outer space.

Successful participants will receive a fully funded opportunity to travel to Geneva, Switzerland, attend the Outer Space Security Conference 2026, and present their work before an audience of diplomats, technical experts, policymakers, military officials, academics, and private-sector leaders.

Application Deadline

Deadline: 21 June 2026

Interested applicants must complete the official submission process before the deadline.

About the Outer Space Security Conference 2026

The Outer Space Security Conference (OS26) is UNIDIR’s flagship annual event focused on the rapidly evolving field of space security. Scheduled to take place on 8–9 September 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland, and online, the conference serves as a global platform where experts and decision-makers discuss emerging threats, opportunities, and policy solutions related to the peaceful use of outer space.

The event brings together a diverse range of stakeholders, including:

  • Diplomats
  • Government representatives
  • Military officials
  • Technical experts
  • Researchers and academics
  • Industry leaders
  • International organizations
  • Civil society representatives

By participating in the Youth Video Competition, young innovators gain a direct pathway into these critical international discussions.

Why This Competition Matters

Outer space has become increasingly important to modern life. Communications, navigation systems, climate monitoring, disaster management, national security operations, and scientific research all depend on space-based infrastructure.

As the number of satellites and space missions continues to grow, so do concerns surrounding:

  • Space sustainability
  • Cybersecurity threats
  • Data integrity
  • Space debris
  • Infrastructure resilience
  • International cooperation
  • Responsible space governance

Recognizing the importance of youth perspectives, UNIDIR and CIGI are creating opportunities for the next generation of scientists, engineers, and researchers to contribute ideas that can strengthen global stability and ensure outer space remains a peaceful and secure environment for all humanity.

The initiative is inspired by the principles of the Outer Space Treaty, which emphasizes that outer space should be explored and used for the benefit of all countries regardless of their level of development and should remain the province of all humankind.

Competition Theme

Participants are invited to submit a 1–3 minute original video addressing the following challenge:

How can innovations in space architecture and technical systems contribute to a more secure, resilient, and sustainable outer space environment?

Applicants are encouraged to present original research demonstrating how updates to space systems can solve modern security and sustainability challenges.

UNIDIR is particularly interested in ideas that address emerging threats and help ensure that critical space assets remain protected from disruption while supporting long-term sustainability.

Suggested Areas of Focus

Applicants may explore a wide variety of technical and research topics, including but not limited to:

1. Shared Technical Standards

  • Common technical protocols
  • Interoperability frameworks
  • International standards for space operations
  • Collaborative infrastructure solutions

2. Cyber-Resilient Space Architecture

  • Satellite cybersecurity
  • Protection against cyberattacks
  • Resilient network design
  • Secure mission operations

3. Data Link Integrity

  • Secure communications systems
  • Anti-jamming technologies
  • Data authentication methods
  • Signal protection innovations

4. Transparency and Confidence-Building Measures

  • Transparency-enhancing technologies
  • Verification mechanisms
  • Trust-building systems for space operations

5. Open-Source Monitoring Frameworks

  • Space situational awareness
  • Open-data platforms
  • Monitoring and tracking systems
  • Public-access analytical tools

6. Innovative Engineering Solutions

  • Sustainable satellite designs
  • Collision avoidance systems
  • Infrastructure resilience improvements
  • New approaches to long-term space sustainability

Participants are encouraged to identify weaknesses within existing systems and propose practical, technically informed solutions that can strengthen global space security.

Competition Objectives

The OS26 Youth Video Competition seeks to:

  • Promote meaningful youth engagement in international space security discussions.
  • Showcase innovative and diverse perspectives on the future of space governance.
  • Encourage emerging leaders to participate in shaping global policy conversations.
  • Highlight technical research that contributes to peaceful and sustainable space activities.
  • Amplify voices from diverse geographical, cultural, and academic backgrounds.

Who Can Apply?

The competition is open to young people worldwide who meet the following criteria:

  • Applicants must be between 18 and 25 years old.
  • Applicants may come from any country.
  • Only one submission is permitted per participant.
  • Videos must be original creations of the entrant.

The organizers strongly encourage submissions representing diverse geographic regions, genders, and perspectives.

Video Requirements

Applicants must ensure their submissions meet all technical and content requirements.

Video Length

  • Minimum: 1 minute
  • Maximum: 3 minutes

File Format

  • Videos must be submitted in .mp4 format.

Audio and Visual Components

The submission must contain:

  • Audio
  • Visual content
  • Subtitles

All elements must be combined into a single file.

Language Requirements

  • Videos may be submitted in any language.
  • Non-English videos must include English closed captions.

Important AI Usage Rules

UNIDIR has established clear guidelines regarding the use of artificial intelligence.

Not Allowed

Participants may not use generative AI to create:

  • The core video narrative
  • The script
  • The presenter’s likeness
  • Fully AI-generated videos

Using AI to replace original creative effort will result in disqualification.

Permitted Uses

AI-assisted tools may be used for:

  • Technical visualizations
  • Engineering schematics
  • Conceptual design renderings
  • Animations demonstrating physical processes
  • Technical illustrations supporting original research

Any AI use must be disclosed in the submission form, and the underlying engineering concepts must remain entirely the participant’s original work.

Selection Criteria

A diverse panel of judges from UNIDIR and CIGI will evaluate submissions based on the following criteria:

1. Originality and Creativity

Judges will assess the uniqueness and innovation of the proposed ideas.

2. Relevance to the Theme

Submissions must clearly connect to space security and sustainability challenges.

3. Clarity and Technical Quality

Ideas should be communicated effectively and supported by sound technical reasoning.

4. Awareness of Space Security Issues

Participants should demonstrate an understanding of current challenges affecting the space environment.

5. Engaging Presentation

Videos should be compelling, thoughtful, and accessible to a broad audience.

The evaluation process has been designed to promote fairness, transparency, and diversity.

Benefits for Selected Winners

The rewards offered through the OS26 Youth Video Competition extend far beyond travel support.

Selected winners will receive:

Fully Funded Participation in Geneva

The award includes:

  • Round-trip economy airfare
  • Accommodation during the conference
  • Per diem support for selected meals

International Exposure

Winners will present their work before a global audience of:

  • Policymakers
  • Diplomats
  • Technical experts
  • Industry leaders
  • Researchers

Conference Presentation Opportunity

Selected videos will be premiered during the conference through dedicated lightning talk sessions.

Global Recognition

Winning videos will be featured on the UNIDIR website and promoted through UNIDIR communication channels.

Publication Opportunity

Selected participants may also have the chance to transform their video project and conference experience into a commentary article for publication on the UNIDIR website.

Intellectual Property and Participation Conditions

By submitting an entry, participants agree that UNIDIR may:

  • Use submitted videos for promotional purposes.
  • Edit videos for editorial use.
  • Include videos in conference compilations.
  • Use participants’ names and likenesses in connection with UNIDIR materials.

Participants should note that submitting a video does not grant permission to use UNIDIR or United Nations logos for personal or commercial purposes.

Why Young Researchers Should Apply

The OS26 Youth Video Competition represents far more than a traditional contest. It offers a rare opportunity to influence international conversations about one of the most important frontiers of the 21st century.

Participants can showcase their technical expertise, contribute original research, gain global visibility, network with leading experts, and help shape future approaches to space security and sustainability.

For ambitious young scientists, engineers, researchers, and innovators interested in the future of space governance, this competition provides a direct platform to share ideas that could contribute to a safer, more resilient, and more sustainable space environment for future generations.

Click here for more and to apply

Final Deadline

21 June 2026

Click here for more global Competition opportunities

 

The post UNIDIR and CIGI Launch the Outer Space Security Conference 2026 Youth Video Competition: Win a Fully Funded Trip to Geneva, Switzerland appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Inclusive AI Innovations in Africa 2026: Apply for Up to USD Equivalent of KES 5 Million to Scale Disability Inclusion Solutions Across Africa
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
The Hub for Artificial Intelligence and Disability Inclusion (HAIDI) Innovation Track has officially launched its Call for Applications for the Inclusive AI Innovations in Africa Programme 2026, offering catalytic funding and long-term support to innovators developing responsible AI solutions that improve the lives of persons with disabilities across Africa. Announced during the Global Data Festival […]

The post Inclusive AI Innovations in Africa 2026: Apply for Up to USD Equivalent of KES 5 Million to Scale Disability Inclusion Solutions Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Hub for Artificial Intelligence and Disability Inclusion (HAIDI) Innovation Track has officially launched its Call for Applications for the Inclusive AI Innovations in Africa Programme 2026, offering catalytic funding and long-term support to innovators developing responsible AI solutions that improve the lives of persons with disabilities across Africa.

Announced during the Global Data Festival 2026, this initiative is designed to identify and support high-potential post-MVP ventures that are already building practical AI-driven solutions capable of creating measurable social impact across the continent.

Selected ventures will receive an intensive 18-month support package that combines funding, technical guidance, community-based user testing, and access to strategic partnerships to help scale inclusive technologies responsibly and sustainably.

Applications close on 17 July 2026.

About the HAIDI Innovation Track

The Hub for Artificial Intelligence and Disability Inclusion (HAIDI) Innovation Track is focused on advancing disability inclusion through the ethical and responsible use of artificial intelligence.

Across Africa, millions of persons with disabilities continue to experience barriers when accessing education, employment, healthcare, caregiving support, digital services, and critical information systems. Many existing technologies are not designed with accessibility or inclusion in mind, limiting opportunities and participation.

Through this programme, HAIDI aims to support innovators who are creating AI-powered solutions that address these challenges while ensuring that disability communities are actively involved in the design, testing, and improvement of these technologies.

The programme prioritizes innovations that are not only technologically strong but also socially impactful, user-centered, and scalable across diverse African contexts.

What Selected Ventures Will Receive

The programme provides far more than just financial support. Selected applicants will gain access to a comprehensive ecosystem designed to help them refine and scale their innovations effectively.

1. Grants of Up to KES 5,000,000

Selected ventures will receive catalytic grants of up to KES 5 million (or USD equivalent) to support the development, testing, and scaling of their AI solutions.

The funding can help ventures:

  • Improve product development
  • Expand accessibility features
  • Strengthen technical infrastructure
  • Conduct pilot implementations
  • Scale operations across new communities
  • Enhance research and validation efforts

2. Hands-On Technical and Product Support

Participants will receive direct technical guidance and product support throughout the 18-month programme.

This includes:

  • Product refinement support
  • Technical mentorship
  • AI development guidance
  • Responsible AI implementation support
  • Scaling and operational advice
  • Product usability improvement strategies

The programme aims to help ventures strengthen both the technical quality and real-world effectiveness of their solutions.

3. User Testing With Disability Communities Across Africa

One of the most important elements of the programme is direct engagement with disability communities.

Selected innovators will have opportunities to:

  • Test their products with real users
  • Gather feedback from persons with disabilities
  • Improve accessibility and usability
  • Validate impact in real-world settings
  • Ensure products are culturally and contextually relevant

This user-centered approach helps ensure that AI solutions are developed responsibly and inclusively rather than in isolation.

4. Access to Partnerships and Future Funding Opportunities

Participants will also gain exposure to a broader ecosystem of partners, experts, and funding networks.

Benefits include:

  • Strategic partnerships
  • Networking opportunities
  • Visibility within the AI and innovation ecosystem
  • Connections with funders and investors
  • Opportunities for future collaborations
  • Access to expert communities and advisors

This support can help ventures continue growing beyond the duration of the programme.

Focus Areas for Innovation

The HAIDI Innovation Track is looking for AI-driven solutions that address challenges affecting persons with disabilities across Africa.

Key areas of interest include:

  • Education
  • Employment
  • Mental health
  • Caregiving
  • Assistive technology
  • Accessibility tools
  • Inclusive communication systems
  • Disability-focused digital services
  • Community empowerment solutions

Innovations from related sectors that contribute to disability inclusion are also encouraged to apply.

Who Can Apply?

The programme welcomes applications from a wide range of innovators and organizations working on inclusive AI technologies.

Eligible applicants include:

  • AI startups and SMEs
  • Individual innovators
  • Researchers
  • Research institutions
  • Universities
  • Multi-stakeholder consortia
  • Post-MVP ventures with existing AI solutions

Applicants should already have a functional solution or product that demonstrates strong potential for scaling and impact.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Artificial intelligence has the potential to significantly improve accessibility, independence, and inclusion for persons with disabilities. However, many AI systems globally are still developed without sufficient representation or accessibility considerations.

The HAIDI Innovation Track aims to change that by supporting African innovators who are building inclusive technologies with disability communities at the center of the process.

This initiative represents a major opportunity for innovators who want to:

  • Build socially impactful AI solutions
  • Scale accessibility-focused technologies
  • Collaborate with disability communities
  • Gain funding and technical support
  • Expand across African markets
  • Strengthen responsible AI practices

By investing in inclusive innovation, the programme seeks to help create a more accessible and equitable digital future across Africa.

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the Inclusive AI Innovations in Africa Programme 2026 is:

17 July 2026

Interested applicants are encouraged to prepare and submit their applications before the deadline.

About the Organizations Behind the Initiative

The AI for Development (AI4D) initiative supports responsible AI research and innovation across Africa.

The initiative is funded by:

  • FCDO
  • IDRC
  • Sida
  • Community Jameel

The programme is supported by the Global Center on AI Governance (GCG).

Final Thoughts

The Inclusive AI Innovations in Africa Programme 2026 is one of the most important opportunities currently available for innovators working at the intersection of artificial intelligence, accessibility, and social impact in Africa.

With substantial grant funding, long-term technical support, community-based testing, and strategic partnerships, the programme provides a powerful platform for scaling inclusive AI solutions that can improve the lives of persons with disabilities across the continent.

Innovators building impactful and responsible AI technologies are strongly encouraged to apply before the 17 July 2026 deadline.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can apply through the official application portal HERE.

Click HERE for more information about the Inclusive AI Innovations in Africa 2026.

Explore more global job opportunities on OFY here.

The post Inclusive AI Innovations in Africa 2026: Apply for Up to USD Equivalent of KES 5 Million to Scale Disability Inclusion Solutions Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Council of Europe Traineeship Programme 2026–2027 in France and Europe: Paid International Internship for Students and Graduates in Human Rights, Democracy, Law, Policy, Communications, IT, HR, and More
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Council of Europe Traineeship Programme 2026–2027, a prestigious international traineeship opportunity designed for students and recent graduates who want to contribute to the promotion of human rights, democracy, and the rule of law across Europe. The Council of Europe is offering approximately 80 traineeship positions for the October 2026–March […]

The post Council of Europe Traineeship Programme 2026–2027 in France and Europe: Paid International Internship for Students and Graduates in Human Rights, Democracy, Law, Policy, Communications, IT, HR, and More appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Council of Europe Traineeship Programme 2026–2027, a prestigious international traineeship opportunity designed for students and recent graduates who want to contribute to the promotion of human rights, democracy, and the rule of law across Europe.

The Council of Europe is offering approximately 80 traineeship positions for the October 2026–March 2027 session, providing participants with a unique opportunity to gain practical experience within one of Europe’s most influential international organizations. Trainees will work alongside experts, policymakers, legal professionals, researchers, and administrative specialists while contributing to meaningful projects that support the Council of Europe’s mission.

The programme offers hands-on professional experience, mentoring, networking opportunities, and financial support, making it one of the most attractive international traineeship opportunities available for young professionals interested in public policy, international affairs, law, governance, communications, human rights, technology, and organizational support services.

About the Council of Europe

The Council of Europe is Europe’s leading organization dedicated to promoting:

  • Human rights
  • Democracy
  • Rule of law

Founded in 1949, the organization works with its member states to strengthen democratic institutions, protect human rights, promote social cohesion, and uphold legal standards across Europe.

The Council of Europe is responsible for many landmark initiatives and institutions, including the European Convention on Human Rights and the European Court of Human Rights.

Through its traineeship programme, the organization seeks to engage talented young people who are passionate about contributing to these values while developing their professional skills in an international environment.

Why Apply for the Council of Europe Traineeship Programme?

The Council of Europe Traineeship Programme is more than just an internship. It is a professional development experience that allows participants to become actively involved in the daily work of an international organization while learning directly from experienced professionals.

Participants gain exposure to:

  • International policy-making
  • Human rights protection
  • Democratic governance
  • International cooperation
  • Public administration
  • Legal affairs
  • Communications
  • Technology and innovation
  • Human resources
  • Organizational management

The programme is designed to help trainees build the skills, knowledge, and professional network needed for future careers in international organizations, government institutions, NGOs, academia, and the private sector.

Number of Traineeship Opportunities

For the 2026–2027 session, approximately:

80 traineeship positions will be available across various Departments and Institutions of the Council of Europe.

Traineeship Duration

The main traineeship session will run:

1 October 2026 – 31 March 2027

Duration:

6 Months

Parliamentary Assembly Traineeships

Some traineeships at the Parliamentary Assembly will follow a different schedule:

1 September 2026 – 28 February 2027

Traineeship Locations

Although most traineeships are based in Strasbourg, France, opportunities are also available in several European cities.

Potential duty stations include:

  • Strasbourg, France
  • Brussels, Belgium
  • Budapest, Hungary
  • Geneva, Switzerland
  • Graz, Austria
  • Lisbon, Portugal
  • Luxembourg
  • The Hague, Netherlands

Applicants should carefully review individual traineeship descriptions to identify available locations.

What Will Trainees Do?

As a Council of Europe trainee, participants become active members of their host teams and contribute to ongoing projects and organizational activities.

Depending on the department selected, trainees may:

Research and Policy Analysis

  • Conduct research on European affairs and public policy issues
  • Analyze legal and political developments
  • Support policy initiatives and programmes
  • Collect and synthesize information for reports

Drafting and Documentation

  • Contribute to studies and reports
  • Draft briefing notes
  • Assist with publications and official documents
  • Prepare background materials for meetings

Meeting and Event Support

  • Help organize international conferences
  • Coordinate committee meetings
  • Support expert consultations
  • Facilitate stakeholder engagement

Communications and Outreach

  • Develop communication materials
  • Create digital content
  • Support social media initiatives
  • Assist with awareness campaigns

Organizational Support Functions

Trainees may also work in support departments including:

  • Human Resources
  • Information Technology
  • Security Services
  • Catering Services
  • Administration
  • Legal Advice
  • Political Affairs

Mentorship and Professional Development

One of the strongest features of the programme is its mentoring component.

Every trainee receives support from an experienced mentor who helps them:

  • Navigate their responsibilities
  • Develop professional skills
  • Understand organizational processes
  • Expand their professional network
  • Maximize learning opportunities

This mentorship ensures trainees gain valuable guidance throughout their placement.

Benefits of the Council of Europe Traineeship

Selected trainees receive a range of benefits.

Financial Support

Trainees in Strasbourg receive approximately:

EUR 1,320 gross per month

Health Insurance

Participants are provided with:

  • Health insurance coverage

Leave Entitlement

Trainees receive:

  • 2.5 days of leave per month worked

Flexible Work Arrangements

Where feasible, trainees may work remotely for:

  • Up to 50% of each two-week period

Professional Benefits

Participants gain:

  • International work experience
  • Access to European experts and policymakers
  • Mentorship support
  • Networking opportunities
  • Exposure to multicultural working environments
  • Professional skill development

Eligibility Criteria

To apply for the Council of Europe Traineeship Programme, applicants must satisfy all eligibility requirements before the application deadline.

Educational Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Have completed at least three years of university studies
  • Hold a Bachelor’s degree or equivalent first-cycle degree under the Bologna Process Framework

Special Note:

For traineeships at the European Court of Human Rights, applicants must have completed:

  • The first year of a Master’s degree

Language Requirements

Candidates must possess:

  • Very good knowledge of English or French

These are the Council of Europe’s official working languages.

Age Requirement

Applicants must be:

  • At least 18 years old

Citizenship Requirement

Candidates must be citizens of a Council of Europe member state.

Previous Participation Restriction

Applicants must not have previously:

  • Completed a Council of Europe traineeship
  • Participated in a Council of Europe study visit
  • Worked for the Council of Europe in any capacity

Family Relationship Restriction

Applicants cannot be:

  • Parent
  • Child
  • Stepchild
  • Grandchild

of a serving Council of Europe staff member.

Skills and Competencies Sought

The Council of Europe values candidates who demonstrate:

Learning and Development

  • Curiosity
  • Adaptability
  • Commitment to growth
  • Continuous learning

Teamwork and Cooperation

  • Collaboration skills
  • Communication abilities
  • Respect for diversity
  • Ability to work in multicultural teams

Application Process

Applicants can indicate:

  • First-choice team
  • Second-choice team

during the application process.

Application Timeline

Applications Open:
4 June 2026

Application Deadline:
17 June 2026

Deadline Time:
23:59:59 CET/CEST

APPLY HERE

You may find out more information on how to fill in and submit an application on the Frequently asked questions page.

Selection Process

The process generally includes:

  1. Online application submission
  2. Eligibility screening
  3. Review by selected teams
  4. Online interview (if shortlisted)
  5. Final selection decision
  6. Notification of results
  7. Signature of traineeship agreement
  8. Start of traineeship

Applicants are typically informed of the outcome approximately two months before the traineeship begins.

For any questions, please contact traineeship@coe.int

Equal Opportunity and Inclusion

The Council of Europe is committed to diversity, inclusion, and equal opportunity.

Applications are welcomed regardless of:

  • Gender
  • Sexual orientation
  • Ethnic origin
  • Social origin
  • Disability
  • Religion
  • Belief

The organization also provides reasonable accommodations for applicants with disabilities or specific needs throughout the recruitment process and traineeship period.

Why This Traineeship Stands Out

Few internship programmes provide the combination of:

  • International exposure
  • Paid professional experience
  • Human rights work
  • Policy engagement
  • Mentorship
  • Networking
  • Multicultural collaboration

available through the Council of Europe Traineeship Programme.

Participants gain first-hand experience supporting one of Europe’s most influential institutions while contributing to meaningful initiatives that shape policies and practices affecting millions of people across the continent.

Click HERE to learn more about the Council of Europe Traineeship Programme 2026–2027 in France and Europe.

Conclusion

The Council of Europe Traineeship Programme 2026–2027 represents an exceptional opportunity for ambitious students and graduates who want to launch an international career while contributing to the promotion of human rights, democracy, and the rule of law. With approximately 80 paid traineeship positions available across multiple European locations, participants will gain invaluable professional experience, mentorship, networking opportunities, and practical skills in a world-class international organization. Whether your interests lie in law, policy, governance, communications, technology, administration, or international cooperation, this programme offers a unique pathway to professional growth and meaningful impact. Aspiring international professionals should take advantage of this opportunity and submit their applications before the 17 June 2026 deadline.

Discover more global Traineeship, Internship, and European opportunities on OFY.

The post Council of Europe Traineeship Programme 2026–2027 in France and Europe: Paid International Internship for Students and Graduates in Human Rights, Democracy, Law, Policy, Communications, IT, HR, and More appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Climate Litigation School 2026: Building the Next Generation of Climate Justice Leaders in Africa
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The Climate Litigation School 2026 is now accepting applications for its upcoming fully funded training programme taking place from 6–10 July 2026 at the Future Africa Campus, University of Pretoria, South Africa. This transformative initiative is jointly hosted by Natural Justice, the Centre for Environmental Rights, and the University of Pretoria’s Centre for Environmental Justice […]

The post Climate Litigation School 2026: Building the Next Generation of Climate Justice Leaders in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Climate Litigation School 2026 is now accepting applications for its upcoming fully funded training programme taking place from 6–10 July 2026 at the Future Africa Campus, University of Pretoria, South Africa. This transformative initiative is jointly hosted by Natural Justice, the Centre for Environmental Rights, and the University of Pretoria’s Centre for Environmental Justice in Africa.

Designed to strengthen the capacity of emerging and established climate justice actors, the School offers an intensive, interdisciplinary learning experience that blends legal strategy, scientific evidence, community advocacy, and rights‑based approaches to climate litigation.

Overview of the Climate Litigation School 2026

The Climate Litigation School is a dedicated training space that brings together students, researchers, lawyers, and climate justice practitioners to deepen their understanding of how law can be used to confront the climate crisis. The programme emphasizes collaboration across disciplines, integrating legal expertise, environmental science, Indigenous knowledge, and community‑driven advocacy.

The 2026 edition of the School will:

  • Equip participants with practical skills in climate litigation.
  • Strengthen interdisciplinary collaboration between lawyers, scientists, activists, and community leaders.
  • Build a network of climate justice advocates across South Africa and the African continent.
  • Promote rights‑based, community‑centered approaches to environmental justice.

The programme is fully funded, covering wheelchair‑accessible accommodation, meals, and flights for selected participants.

Programme Dates and Location

  • Curriculum Dates: 6 – 10 July 2026
  • Venue: Future Africa Campus, University of Pretoria, South Africa
  • Funding: Fully funded (accommodation, meals, and flights covered)
  • Application Deadline: 16 June 2026

About the Climate Litigation School

The School is designed to build long‑term capacity among individuals working to advance climate justice. It goes beyond traditional legal training by focusing on real‑world, context‑specific challenges and opportunities in climate litigation.

Key Focus Areas

  1. Climate Litigation Strategies Participants will explore landmark climate cases, strategic litigation approaches, and emerging legal tools used to hold governments and corporations accountable.
  2. Environmental Governance The curriculum examines how governance systems shape environmental decision‑making and how legal interventions can strengthen accountability.
  3. Climate Science Integration The School emphasizes the importance of climate attribution science, environmental data, and impact projections in building strong legal cases.
  4. Community‑Lawyer Alliances A core component is the integration of community voices, ensuring that frontline communities are co‑creators of legal strategies.
  5. Indigenous and Traditional Knowledge The programme highlights the role of Indigenous knowledge systems in shaping culturally grounded, rights‑based litigation.

What Makes This School Unique

The Climate Litigation School stands out for its holistic, interdisciplinary approach. It brings together:

  • Legal practitioners
  • Environmental scientists
  • Indigenous knowledge holders
  • Community leaders
  • Climate justice activists
  • Policy researchers

This collaborative model ensures that litigation strategies are not only technically sound but also socially just, culturally relevant, and grounded in lived realities.

Participants will engage with leading experts working at the forefront of climate litigation, gaining insights into both national and continental climate justice movements.

Who Should Apply

Applications are welcomed from individuals who:

  • Are based in South Africa
  • Are working or studying in fields such as:
    • Law
    • Environmental science
    • Public policy
    • Climate justice activism
  • Are committed to applying the knowledge gained to support climate justice in their communities or professional work

Selection Criteria

Applicants will be evaluated based on:

  • Demonstrated interest or experience in climate, environmental, or human rights work
  • Potential to contribute to and benefit from the programme
  • Commitment to advancing climate justice
  • Consideration of diversity, including racial and gender representation

How to Apply

Interested applicants can submit their applications through the official application portal. Deadline: 16 June 2026

Themes and Programme Areas

  • Legal Empowerment
  • Litigation
  • Climate Change
  • Environmental Justice
  • Defending Rights

Related Topics for Further Exploration

  • High Court seismic survey challenge
  • Africa’s energy transition delays
  • Deep-water drilling court hearings
  • Community power and climate justice

Apply here

For more information about this opportunity visit here

Discover more  fellowship opportunities here 

The post Climate Litigation School 2026: Building the Next Generation of Climate Justice Leaders in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Heinrich Böll Foundation Scholarships 2026–2027 in Germany: Fully Funded Opportunities for Master’s and PhD Students from DAC Countries
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The Heinrich Böll Foundation offers prestigious scholarship opportunities for highly motivated international students and doctoral candidates who wish to pursue higher education in Germany. These scholarships are designed to promote academic excellence, social engagement, democratic values, and environmental sustainability. The programme primarily targets talented students and researchers from DAC (Development Assistance Committee) countries who aim […]

The post Heinrich Böll Foundation Scholarships 2026–2027 in Germany: Fully Funded Opportunities for Master’s and PhD Students from DAC Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Heinrich Böll Foundation offers prestigious scholarship opportunities for highly motivated international students and doctoral candidates who wish to pursue higher education in Germany. These scholarships are designed to promote academic excellence, social engagement, democratic values, and environmental sustainability.

The programme primarily targets talented students and researchers from DAC (Development Assistance Committee) countries who aim to pursue a Master’s degree or PhD in Germany. The foundation supports individuals who demonstrate strong academic performance, political awareness, and commitment to social justice, human rights, and environmental responsibility.

This scholarship initiative is funded by the Federal Ministry of Education and Research (BMBF) and the Federal Foreign Office (AA), making it one of the most reputable fully supported academic funding opportunities in Germany.

About the Heinrich Böll Foundation

The Heinrich Böll Foundation is a German political foundation affiliated with the Green political movement. It is committed to:

  • Promoting democracy and human rights globally
  • Supporting environmental sustainability and climate justice
  • Encouraging gender equality and social inclusion
  • Strengthening civic education and political participation
  • Supporting international academic exchange and research development

Through its scholarship programme, the foundation builds a global network of young leaders who contribute to progressive social change.

Scholarship Overview: Tailwind for Talents Programme 2026–2027

The “Tailwind for Talents” scholarship supports international students and PhD researchers who plan to study in Germany. The programme provides financial assistance, academic support, and access to a strong global alumni network.

Key Details

  • Scholarship Provider: Heinrich Böll Foundation
  • Country: Germany
  • Study Level: Master’s and PhD
  • Application Periods:
    • Fall 2026: 15 July 2026 – 1 September 2026
    • Spring 2027: 15 January 2027 – 1 March 2027
  • Funding Type: Fully funded scholarship support (financial assistance and academic support)

Who Can Apply?

The scholarship is open to two main categories of applicants:

1. Master’s Students

Applicants must:

  • Have completed their first university degree outside Germany
  • Be planning to pursue a Master’s degree in Germany
  • Not have started living in Germany at the time of application
  • Come from DAC countries (priority group)

2. PhD Candidates

Applicants must:

  • Have completed a graduate degree abroad
  • Be applying for or planning to pursue a PhD programme in Germany
  • Not be residing in Germany at the time of application
  • Be international applicants from DAC countries (priority priority group)

Priority Regions: DAC Countries

Priority is given to applicants from DAC-listed developing countries. These typically include regions in:

  • Africa
  • Asia
  • Latin America and the Caribbean
  • Parts of the Middle East and Pacific regions

This ensures the scholarship supports students from underrepresented and developing regions.

Fields of Study

While open to many academic disciplines, strong preference is often given to candidates studying:

  • Political Science
  • International Relations
  • Environmental Studies
  • Law and Human Rights
  • Social Sciences
  • Economics and Development Studies
  • Climate Policy and Sustainability-related fields

Scholarship Benefits

The Heinrich Böll Foundation Scholarship offers more than financial support. It provides a complete academic and professional development ecosystem.

Key Benefits Include:

  • Financial support for living and academic expenses
  • Access to a strong academic and professional network in Germany
  • Participation in seminars, workshops, and training programmes
  • Integration into a global alumni community
  • Career development and leadership training opportunities
  • Exposure to German political, environmental, and social systems

Application Process

Applicants are required to complete and submit official application forms depending on their study level:

  • Application for Master’s students (PDF form)
  • Application for PhD candidates (PDF form)

Additional Requirements:

Applicants are typically expected to provide:

  • Academic transcripts and certificates
  • Motivation letter
  • Research proposal (for PhD applicants)
  • Proof of language proficiency (depending on programme requirements)
  • Curriculum vitae (CV)

Selection Criteria

Candidates are assessed based on:

  • Academic excellence
  • Social and political engagement
  • Motivation and clarity of study/research goals
  • Commitment to democracy, sustainability, and human rights
  • Leadership potential and civic responsibility

Why This Scholarship Matters

The Heinrich Böll Foundation Scholarship is not just financial aid—it is a leadership development platform. It enables scholars to:

  • Study in one of Europe’s strongest academic systems
  • Join a global network of change-makers
  • Engage in environmental and social policy discussions
  • Build long-term careers in academia, governance, NGOs, and international organizations

Application Deadlines

  • Fall 2026 intake: 15 July 2026 – 1 September 2026
  • Spring 2027 intake: 15 January 2027 – 1 March 2027

Late applications are not accepted.

Learn more and apply here

Conclusion

The Heinrich Böll Foundation Scholarship 2026–2027 is an excellent opportunity for ambitious international students and researchers who want to study in Germany while contributing to global social, environmental, and political change. It is highly competitive and ideal for individuals committed to academic excellence and progressive leadership.

For more information, visit the official website. 

Discover more fully funded global scholarships on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Heinrich Böll Foundation Scholarships 2026–2027 in Germany: Fully Funded Opportunities for Master’s and PhD Students from DAC Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: WFF Academic Engagement 2026 Applications Open: Connect Your Research to Global Food Policy and Agrifood Systems Transformation
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Universities, educators, researchers, and students around the world now have an exciting opportunity to engage directly with global policy discussions and contribute to the future of agrifood systems. The World Food Forum (WFF) Youth Initiative has officially opened applications for the 2026 WFF Academic Engagement Programme, inviting academic institutions and their communities to participate in […]

The post WFF Academic Engagement 2026 Applications Open: Connect Your Research to Global Food Policy and Agrifood Systems Transformation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Universities, educators, researchers, and students around the world now have an exciting opportunity to engage directly with global policy discussions and contribute to the future of agrifood systems. The World Food Forum (WFF) Youth Initiative has officially opened applications for the 2026 WFF Academic Engagement Programme, inviting academic institutions and their communities to participate in one of the world’s leading youth-driven platforms for food systems transformation.

The programme offers a unique opportunity for students and faculty members to bridge the gap between academic research and real-world policy impact by engaging with global stakeholders, international experts, and policymakers working to address some of the most pressing challenges facing food systems today.

Whether you are a student seeking to bring your research into international policy conversations, a faculty member interested in connecting classroom learning to practical global challenges, or a university aiming to strengthen its international presence and impact, the WFF Academic Engagement Programme provides a powerful platform for participation.

Application Deadline

Deadline: 24 June 2026

Interested institutions and participants should submit their applications before the closing date.

What is the World Food Forum (WFF)?

The World Food Forum (WFF) is a global youth-led movement and network designed to accelerate action toward transforming agrifood systems. Established as an initiative of the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO), the WFF serves as a platform where young people, researchers, innovators, policymakers, educators, and development practitioners come together to address global food security and sustainability challenges.

The forum promotes collaboration, innovation, education, and policy engagement to create more sustainable, inclusive, resilient, and equitable agrifood systems worldwide.

Through its various programmes and initiatives, the WFF empowers youth to contribute meaningful solutions to global challenges such as:

  • Food insecurity
  • Climate change
  • Sustainable agriculture
  • Rural development
  • Agricultural innovation
  • Nutrition and public health
  • Food systems resilience
  • Biodiversity conservation
  • Sustainable development

What is the WFF Academic Engagement Programme?

The WFF Academic Engagement Programme is designed to connect higher education institutions with the World Food Forum’s global activities and policy discussions.

The programme enables students and educators to actively engage in international conversations surrounding agrifood systems while applying academic knowledge to real-world challenges.

By participating, universities and academic communities gain opportunities to contribute research, share innovative ideas, and collaborate with global experts working on sustainable food systems transformation.

The initiative strengthens the connection between academia and policy, ensuring that emerging research and youth perspectives help inform decision-making processes at local, national, and international levels.

Why Academic Engagement Matters

Universities play a critical role in generating knowledge, developing innovative solutions, and preparing future leaders.

However, academic research often remains disconnected from policymaking processes and implementation efforts. The WFF Academic Engagement Programme addresses this gap by creating opportunities for students and faculty members to engage directly with global stakeholders and contribute to discussions that influence the future of food systems.

Participation helps transform classroom learning into practical action while providing valuable international exposure.

Participation Opportunities

The 2026 WFF Academic Engagement Programme offers multiple pathways for involvement.

1. Academic Delegations

Universities can form official academic delegations that participate in World Food Forum activities and events.

Academic delegations provide opportunities for:

  • Student participation in international discussions
  • Faculty engagement with policymakers and experts
  • Institutional representation at global events
  • Networking with universities worldwide
  • Knowledge exchange across disciplines and regions

2. Course-Integrated Participation

Faculty members can integrate WFF activities into academic courses, allowing students to engage directly with real-world agrifood systems challenges as part of their learning experience.

Benefits include:

  • Experiential learning opportunities
  • Applied research experiences
  • Enhanced student engagement
  • Global policy exposure
  • Practical problem-solving exercises
  • International collaboration opportunities

Key Benefits of Participating

The WFF Academic Engagement Programme offers substantial benefits for students, educators, and institutions alike.

Benefits for Students

Students who participate can:

  • Engage with global policy consultations
  • Connect academic research to real-world challenges
  • Gain international exposure
  • Build professional networks
  • Interact with FAO experts and practitioners
  • Strengthen leadership and communication skills
  • Explore international career pathways
  • Contribute to global food systems transformation

Benefits for Faculty Members

Educators and researchers can:

  • Integrate global policy engagement into coursework
  • Expand international collaborations
  • Connect research outputs with policy discussions
  • Engage with multidisciplinary experts
  • Increase the visibility of academic work
  • Enhance experiential learning opportunities for students

Benefits for Universities

Academic institutions can:

  • Strengthen their global reputation
  • Demonstrate commitment to sustainability and innovation
  • Increase international visibility
  • Expand institutional partnerships
  • Showcase research excellence
  • Position themselves as leaders in agrifood systems transformation
  • Create new opportunities for students and faculty

Opportunities to Engage with Global Policy Processes

One of the programme’s most valuable aspects is direct engagement with international policy conversations.

Participants will have opportunities to:

Engage with the WFF Youth Assembly

The WFF Youth Assembly serves as a platform where young leaders discuss challenges and solutions related to food systems transformation.

Participants can contribute perspectives, share research findings, and collaborate with peers from around the world.

Participate in Global Policy Consultations

The programme enables participants to engage with policy discussions addressing critical issues affecting agriculture, food security, sustainability, and rural development.

This exposure helps students understand how research informs policy and decision-making processes.

Collaborate with FAO Experts

Participants gain access to technical experts from the Food and Agriculture Organization and other leading organizations working in agrifood systems.

These interactions provide valuable insights into emerging challenges, best practices, and career opportunities.

Building a Global Network

The programme creates opportunities to join a diverse international community committed to advancing sustainable agrifood systems.

Participants will connect with:

  • Students from different countries
  • Faculty members and educators
  • Researchers and scholars
  • Policymakers
  • Development practitioners
  • Industry leaders
  • FAO technical experts
  • Youth advocates and innovators

These connections often lead to long-term collaborations, research partnerships, internships, and career opportunities.

Recognition and Visibility

Participation in the WFF Academic Engagement Programme can significantly enhance both individual and institutional visibility.

Benefits may include:

  • International recognition
  • Showcasing academic projects and research
  • Increased exposure to global audiences
  • Opportunities to highlight innovative solutions
  • Enhanced professional and institutional profiles

For universities seeking to expand their global engagement, participation offers a valuable opportunity to demonstrate leadership in sustainability, innovation, and youth development.

Why Universities Should Apply

The future of food systems will be shaped by today’s researchers, students, educators, and innovators.

By participating in the 2026 WFF Academic Engagement Programme, universities can position themselves at the forefront of agrifood systems transformation while empowering their students and faculty to contribute to meaningful global change.

The programme offers a unique combination of policy engagement, international collaboration, academic enrichment, and professional development that can generate lasting impact for participants and institutions alike.

As food security, sustainability, and climate resilience continue to emerge as global priorities, engaging with platforms such as the World Food Forum allows academic institutions to play a central role in shaping solutions for future generations.

Application Link

Click here for more and to apply

Final Deadline

24 June 2026

Click here for more global fellowship opportunities

 

The post WFF Academic Engagement 2026 Applications Open: Connect Your Research to Global Food Policy and Agrifood Systems Transformation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: William P. Fuller Fellowship in Peacebuilding 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Opportunity for Emerging Peacebuilders Across Asia and the Pacific
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Applications are now open for the 2026 William P. Fuller Fellowship in Peacebuilding, a prestigious leadership development programme offered by The Asia Foundation (TAF) for emerging peacebuilders from Asia and the Pacific. Designed to support the next generation of leaders working in peacebuilding, conflict transformation, mediation, advocacy, policy, and community engagement, the fellowship provides a […]

The post William P. Fuller Fellowship in Peacebuilding 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Opportunity for Emerging Peacebuilders Across Asia and the Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the 2026 William P. Fuller Fellowship in Peacebuilding, a prestigious leadership development programme offered by The Asia Foundation (TAF) for emerging peacebuilders from Asia and the Pacific. Designed to support the next generation of leaders working in peacebuilding, conflict transformation, mediation, advocacy, policy, and community engagement, the fellowship provides a year-long professional development experience that combines international exposure, leadership training, networking, and project support.

Each year, the fellowship selects a small cohort of exceptional professionals who have demonstrated a commitment to advancing peace and addressing conflict challenges in their communities and countries. Through immersive learning experiences, engagement with global experts, and dedicated professional development funding, the fellowship equips participants with the knowledge, networks, and skills needed to strengthen their impact.

For 2026, three fellows will be selected to participate in this highly competitive programme, which includes a two-week study tour in the United States, virtual leadership development seminars, and a professional development grant worth USD 2,500.

About the William P. Fuller Fellowship in Peacebuilding

The William P. Fuller Fellowship in Peacebuilding is The Asia Foundation’s flagship leadership development initiative focused on peacebuilding and conflict transformation.

Established in 2004 by The Asia Foundation’s Board of Trustees, the fellowship honors William P. Fuller, who served as President of The Asia Foundation for 15 years. The programme reflects his commitment to nurturing emerging leaders dedicated to peace, stability, and social cohesion throughout Asia and the Pacific.

The fellowship is supported through privately endowed funds and seeks to empower professionals whose work contributes to building peaceful, inclusive, and resilient societies.

As conflict dynamics continue to evolve throughout the region, there is increasing demand for leaders capable of:

  • Bridging social, political, and cultural divides
  • Facilitating dialogue among diverse stakeholders
  • Promoting peaceful conflict resolution
  • Designing locally grounded peacebuilding solutions
  • Strengthening social cohesion
  • Supporting reconciliation processes
  • Addressing root causes of conflict

The Fuller Fellowship aims to strengthen the leadership capacity of individuals already working in these areas.

Why This Fellowship Matters

The Asia-Pacific region continues to experience complex conflict and peacebuilding challenges, including:

  • Political instability
  • Social fragmentation
  • Identity-based conflicts
  • Community-level disputes
  • Governance challenges
  • Resource conflicts
  • Emerging security concerns

Addressing these issues requires innovative leaders who can combine research, policy engagement, community-based approaches, and collaborative problem-solving.

The William P. Fuller Fellowship supports such leaders by helping them expand their influence, strengthen their networks, and gain access to global best practices in peacebuilding.

What the Fellowship Offers

The fellowship provides a comprehensive leadership development experience lasting one year.

1. Two-Week Study Tour in the United States

One of the most valuable components of the fellowship is an intensive study tour and professional exchange programme.

Selected fellows will travel to:

  • San Francisco Bay Area
  • New York City
  • Washington, D.C.

Study Tour Dates

10–21 November 2026

During the visit, fellows will engage with:

  • Peacebuilding practitioners
  • Policymakers
  • Academic experts
  • Civil society leaders
  • Researchers
  • Government representatives
  • International development professionals

The study tour provides exposure to diverse approaches to peacebuilding and conflict transformation from both local and global perspectives.

2. Leadership Development Seminars

Fellows will participate in virtual learning sessions delivered through The Asia Foundation’s Leadership & Exchange Programmes.

Topics may include:

  • Leadership development
  • Peacebuilding strategies
  • Conflict transformation
  • Negotiation and mediation
  • Policy engagement
  • Systems thinking
  • Strategic communication
  • Stakeholder engagement

3. Professional Development Grant

Each fellow receives:

USD 2,500 Professional Development Grant

The grant can be used to support:

  • A self-designed project
  • Professional learning goals
  • Research initiatives
  • Leadership development activities
  • Community engagement projects
  • Capacity-building efforts

This funding enables fellows to pursue initiatives that strengthen their long-term impact.

4. International Professional Network

Participants gain access to an influential network of:

  • Peacebuilding experts
  • Regional leaders
  • Civil society practitioners
  • Researchers
  • Policymakers
  • Alumni fellows

This network often becomes a long-term source of collaboration and professional support.

Fellowship Objectives

The programme seeks to:

  1. Strengthen leadership skills among emerging peacebuilders.
  2. Expand professional networks across Asia and the Pacific.
  3. Support innovative approaches to conflict transformation.
  4. Encourage cross-border collaboration.
  5. Promote dialogue and mutual understanding.
  6. Advance sustainable peacebuilding efforts throughout the region.
  7. Develop future leaders capable of responding to evolving conflict challenges.

Who Should Apply?

The fellowship targets early- and mid-career professionals actively contributing to peacebuilding and conflict transformation.

Applicants may be working in areas such as:

  • Research
  • Public policy
  • Mediation
  • Community engagement
  • Peace advocacy
  • Dialogue facilitation
  • Human rights
  • Conflict resolution
  • Social cohesion
  • Governance
  • Development programming

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for the William P. Fuller Fellowship in Peacebuilding 2026, applicants must meet the following requirements:

Age Requirement

Applicants must be:

  • 40 years old or younger during the programme year
  • Born after 13 October 1986

Geographic Eligibility

Applicants must come from a country where The Asia Foundation has a presence.

U.S. Travel Eligibility

Applicants must be able to secure a visa to travel to the United States.

Special note:

Candidates from:

  • Myanmar
  • Afghanistan
  • Laos

must currently be based in the United States.

Language Requirement

Applicants must be fluent in:

  • Written English
  • Spoken English

Professional Experience

Strong candidates typically have:

  • At least three years of professional experience
  • Demonstrated leadership capacity
  • Experience advancing peacebuilding efforts through research or practice

Commitment Requirement

Applicants must be able to commit fully to all fellowship activities and programme dates.

Characteristics of Strong Candidates

The Asia Foundation is particularly interested in candidates who:

  • Demonstrate leadership potential
  • Are committed to peacebuilding in the Asia-Pacific region
  • Value collaboration and learning
  • Seek to expand professional networks
  • Show initiative and innovation
  • Are willing to complete self-directed learning
  • Have a clear vision for their future contributions to peacebuilding

Programme Impact

Since its creation, the fellowship has helped develop a growing network of peacebuilding leaders throughout Asia and the Pacific.

Participants often go on to:

  • Lead peace initiatives
  • Influence policy reforms
  • Conduct impactful research
  • Strengthen civil society organizations
  • Facilitate community dialogue
  • Build regional partnerships

The fellowship serves as a platform for long-term leadership development and collaboration.

Application Timeline

Applications Open

10 June 2026

Applications Close

12 July 2026

Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications early and prepare supporting materials well before the deadline.

APPLY HERE

Why You Should Apply

The William P. Fuller Fellowship in Peacebuilding is one of the most prestigious opportunities available for emerging peacebuilders in Asia and the Pacific.

The fellowship offers far more than professional training. It provides:

  • International exposure
  • Leadership development
  • Global networking opportunities
  • Access to leading experts
  • Financial support for professional growth
  • Long-term membership in a regional peacebuilding network

For professionals dedicated to promoting peace, dialogue, and conflict transformation, this fellowship offers a unique opportunity to strengthen their leadership journey and amplify their impact.

Click HERE for more information about the William P. Fuller Fellowship in Peacebuilding 2026.

Conclusion

The 2026 William P. Fuller Fellowship in Peacebuilding represents an exceptional opportunity for emerging leaders working to build more peaceful and inclusive societies across Asia and the Pacific. Through international exchanges, leadership development, mentorship, networking opportunities, and financial support, the fellowship equips participants with the tools needed to address complex peace and conflict challenges in their communities. As societies across the region navigate increasingly complex social and political dynamics, the need for innovative and collaborative peacebuilders has never been greater. This fellowship offers a powerful platform for rising leaders to expand their influence, strengthen their expertise, and contribute meaningfully to lasting peace.

Discover more global Fellowship and Asian opportunities on OFY.

The post William P. Fuller Fellowship in Peacebuilding 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Opportunity for Emerging Peacebuilders Across Asia and the Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNIDIR Graduate Professional Programme 2026: Security and Technology (Digital Tools) in Geneva/Remote
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The United Nations Institute for Disarmament Research (UNIDIR) is offering an exceptional opportunity for early-career professionals and recent graduates through its Graduate Professional Programme (GPP) 2026. This position focuses on Security and Technology, specifically supporting Digital Tools such as the Cyber Policy Portal and Artificial Intelligence Policy Portal. Based in Geneva, Switzerland, with remote participation […]

The post UNIDIR Graduate Professional Programme 2026: Security and Technology (Digital Tools) in Geneva/Remote appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations Institute for Disarmament Research (UNIDIR) is offering an exceptional opportunity for early-career professionals and recent graduates through its Graduate Professional Programme (GPP) 2026. This position focuses on Security and Technology, specifically supporting Digital Tools such as the Cyber Policy Portal and Artificial Intelligence Policy Portal.

Based in Geneva, Switzerland, with remote participation options available, this 6-month role provides a structured entry point into international peace and security work within the United Nations system. The programme is designed for individuals passionate about emerging technologies, digital governance, cybersecurity policy, and global disarmament issues.

The Graduate Professional will work under the UNIDIR Security and Technology Programme, contributing directly to research support systems, communication tools, and policy knowledge platforms used by international stakeholders.

About UNIDIR and Its Mission

The United Nations Institute for Disarmament Research (UNIDIR) is an autonomous UN institute funded voluntarily and based in Geneva. It plays a critical role in:

  • Producing independent research on disarmament and global security
  • Facilitating international dialogue on emerging security threats
  • Supporting policy development on technological risks in security environments
  • Promoting innovative solutions to global peace and stability challenges

The Security and Technology Programme focuses on understanding how technological advancements impact international security, including:

  • Artificial intelligence governance
  • Cybersecurity policy frameworks
  • Digital transformation in security systems
  • Emerging technologies and global risk mitigation

Position Overview: Graduate Professional (Security and Technology – Digital Tools)

This role is designed to support the development, maintenance, and improvement of UNIDIR’s digital knowledge platforms, including:

  • Cyber Policy Portal
